name: Veritas
description: Veritas, in Roman mythology, is the goddess of truth. She is the daughter of Saturn, the god of agriculture, generation, dissolution, plenty, wealth, agriculture, periodic renewal and liberation, and also time. Veritas is often depicted as a young virgin dressed in white, symbolizing purity and honesty. She is sometimes shown holding a hand mirror, representing the reflection of truth, or standing near a well, representing truth hidden deep within. Her role was to uphold honesty, justice, and integrity in both human affairs and divine matters. She was seen as essential for a well-ordered society and was invoked in oaths and legal proceedings to ensure that people spoke truthfully. While not as widely worshipped as other major Roman deities, Veritas held significant importance as a personification of a core value in Roman society. Her influence extended to concepts of moral virtue and the pursuit of knowledge. The temple dedicated to her was considered a place where truth could be sought and found. Veritas embodies the Roman ideal of 'virtus,' representing moral excellence and integrity. Her presence served as a constant reminder of the importance of honesty and ethical behavior in all aspects of life. The pursuit of Veritas was considered a noble and essential endeavor for all citizens, reflecting the Roman emphasis on law, order, and justice. She remains a powerful symbol of truth and integrity to this day.
subtitle: Goddess of Truth and Honesty

keyMyths.title: Veritas' Birth
keyMyths.description: Veritas, daughter of Saturn, embodies the essence of truth from her very origin. Her birth symbolizes the inherent value of honesty in the fabric of the cosmos.

keyMyths.title: The Well of Veritas
keyMyths.description: It is said that Veritas resides near a well, where the deepest truths are hidden. Seeking her guidance requires a journey into the depths of one's self.

keyMyths.title: Veritas and Justice
keyMyths.description: Veritas is closely associated with Justice, ensuring fair judgments and upholding the law. Her presence guarantees honesty in legal proceedings.

sources.author: Marcus Tullius Cicero
sources.description: Cicero's work discusses moral duties and virtues, relevant to Veritas' domain.
sources.text: Cicero's work discusses moral duties and virtues, relevant to Veritas' domain.
